winner.title: Delegation circuit does not prove delegator is enrolled — phantom delegator attack
winner.description: The Delegation circuit takes `delegatorCredCommitment` as a private input and verifies the delegator's EdDSA signature over the delegation token, but never proves that `delegatorCredCommitment` exists in the agent Merkle tree. The chain-link is via `previousScopeCommitment` (a public input matched on-chain), but a first-hop delegation from a fabricated agent whose credential was never enrolled can produce a valid `previousScopeCommitment` if the attacker controls the AgentPolicy proof (e.g., in a compromised relayer scenario where the relayer submits both proofs). Fix: add a Merkle inclusion proof for `delegatorCredCommitment` against the agent tree root, similar to the existing delegatee inclusion check (CIP-1). Cost: one additional BinaryMerkleRoot(20) (~6k constraints).

outline.title: Delegation circuit does not prove delegator is enrolled — phantom delegator attack

artifacts.description: Add Merkle inclusion proof for delegatorCredCommitment against agentTreeRoot; introduce agentTreeRoot as a new public input and delegatorMerklePath/delegatorMerklePathIndices as private inputs; instantiate BinaryMerkleRoot(20) component and assert root equality.
Existing shared Merkle component reused for the delegator inclusion check — no changes needed, confirm depth-20 parameterization is compatible.
Regenerated Groth16/PLONK verifier from updated Delegation.circom vkey after re-running trusted setup; public signal array gains one additional element (agentTreeRoot).
Update verifyDelegation() call-site to pass agentTreeRoot as part of the public inputs array; enforce that agentTreeRoot matches the on-chain committed agent tree root.
Extend DelegationInputs type to include delegatorMerklePath and delegatorMerklePathIndices; update proveDelegation() to fetch and include these Merkle witness fields before calling snarkjs.
Document CIP-1: delegator enrollment requirement; add agentTreeRoot to Delegation proof public signal table; describe phantom delegator attack vector and mitigation.
Add formal property: delegatorCredCommitment ∈ agentTree(agentTreeRoot); reference new constraint count and BinaryMerkleRoot depth.

Add fast witness tests: (1) valid delegator path passes; (2) fabricated delegatorCredCommitment not in tree fails constraint; (3) mismatched agentTreeRoot fails. Add slow proof test for full Groth16 round-trip with delegator inclusion.
Add integration test: submit delegation proof with phantom delegator (credential not enrolled) — expect revert; submit with enrolled delegator — expect success.

steps: Audit Delegation.circom to identify all existing public/private signals and document current constraint count baseline.
Add agentTreeRoot as a new public input to Delegation.circom.
Add delegatorMerklePath[20] and delegatorMerklePathIndices[20] as private inputs.
Instantiate BinaryMerkleRoot(20) component; wire delegatorCredCommitment as leaf and delegatorMerklePath/delegatorMerklePathIndices as siblings/path; assert computed root === agentTreeRoot.
Re-run trusted setup: npm run compile:circuits to regenerate .r1cs, then snarkjs groth16 setup and plonk setup against pot16.ptau to produce new .zkey files for Delegation.
Export new vkey.json and regenerate DelegationVerifier.sol via snarkjs generateverifier; replace the existing verifier contract.
Update BolyraRegistry.sol verifyDelegation() to include agentTreeRoot in the public signals array and validate it against the on-chain committed root.
Extend DelegationInputs TypeScript type with Merkle witness fields; update proveDelegation() in sdk/src/delegation.ts to accept and pass these fields to snarkjs.groth16.fullProve.
Write failing circuit tests (witness-only) covering phantom delegator rejection, valid delegator acceptance, and root mismatch rejection.
Run npm run test:circuits:fast — confirm new tests fail on unpatched circuit, then pass after patch.
Write Hardhat integration tests in contracts/test/BolyraRegistry.test.js covering phantom and enrolled delegator paths.
Run npm run test:contracts — confirm all pass.
Run npm run test:circuits:slow (FULL_PROOF=1) for full Groth16 proof round-trip on Delegation.
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md public signals table and security considerations section with CIP-1 phantom delegator attack description.
Update circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md with new formal property and revised constraint count.
Commit with DCO sign-off: git commit -s -m 'fix(circuits): add delegator Merkle inclusion proof to Delegation circuit (CIP-1)'
dependencies: BinaryMerkleRoot(20) component already in circuits/src/
pot16.ptau (2^16 constraints) — verify headroom after adding ~6k constraints
snarkjs for re-running trusted setup and verifier export
Existing agentTreeRoot on-chain commitment in BolyraRegistry (must be stored/accessible at delegation verification time)
SDK Merkle witness generation utility (getMerkleProof against agent tree) — may need to be added to sdk/src/merkle.ts if not present

winner.title: Add under-constrained Merkle depth range check
winner.description: All three circuits pass merkleProofLength to BinaryMerkleRoot(MAX_DEPTH) but do not range-check that merkleProofLength <= MAX_DEPTH or merkleProofLength >= 1. If merkleProofLength is 0 or exceeds MAX_DEPTH, BinaryMerkleRoot may silently produce a root from an empty or truncated path. Add explicit Num2Bits + LessThan constraints ensuring 1 <= merkleProofLength <= MAX_DEPTH in HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation, and ModelInstanceBinding. Write a witness-generation test that attempts merkleProofLength = 0 and merkleProofLength = MAX_DEPTH + 1 and asserts constraint failure.

outline.title: Add under-constrained Merkle depth range check

artifacts.description: Add Num2Bits + LessThan range-check constraints ensuring 1 <= merkleProofLength <= MAX_DEPTH before passing to BinaryMerkleRoot
Same merkleProofLength range-check constraints as HumanUniqueness
Same merkleProofLength range-check constraints as HumanUniqueness
Same merkleProofLength range-check constraints if this circuit uses BinaryMerkleRoot; add if present
Witness-generation tests asserting constraint failure for merkleProofLength=0 and merkleProofLength=MAX_DEPTH+1 across all affected circuits

steps: Audit all four circuits (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation, ModelInstanceBinding) to confirm each passes merkleProofLength as a signal to BinaryMerkleRoot and identify the MAX_DEPTH constant in each
In each circuit, declare a Num2Bits(log2(MAX_DEPTH+1)) component fed by merkleProofLength to implicitly bound the signal bit-width and prevent overflow
Add a LessThan(log2(MAX_DEPTH+1)) component comparing merkleProofLength against MAX_DEPTH+1; constrain its output === 1 to enforce merkleProofLength <= MAX_DEPTH
Add a second LessThan(log2(MAX_DEPTH+1)) component comparing 0 against merkleProofLength; constrain its output === 1 to enforce merkleProofLength >= 1
Wire the range-check outputs as hard constraints (=== 1) before the BinaryMerkleRoot instantiation to ensure the prover cannot bypass them by witness manipulation
Recompile all modified circuits with `npm run compile:circuits` and confirm R1CS constraint counts increase by ~4-6 constraints per circuit

Write circuits/test/merkleDepthGuard.test.js with four test cases: (HumanUniqueness, merkleProofLength=0), (HumanUniqueness, merkleProofLength=MAX_DEPTH+1), plus analogous cases for at least one other circuit; each must assert that witness generation throws a constraint-failure error
Run `npm run test:circuits:fast` to confirm the new tests pass (constraint failures are detected during witness generation without needing full proof)
Update circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md to document the new invariant: 'merkleProofLength is range-checked to [1, MAX_DEPTH] via LessThan constraints prior to BinaryMerkleRoot instantiation'
Verify no existing fast or slow circuit tests regress; run `npm run test:circuits:slow` on CI or locally to confirm end-to-end proof generation still succeeds with valid inputs
dependencies: circomlib LessThan component (already a project dependency via circomlib)
circomlib Num2Bits component (already a project dependency via circomlib)
BinaryMerkleRoot component interface must expose merkleProofLength as a distinct signal (confirm it is not internally derived)
circuits/build/ must be regenerated after circuit changes before running slow proof tests

pot16.ptau and existing .zkey artifacts — re-trusted-setup not required if only adding constraints to non-HumanUniqueness circuits; HumanUniqueness reuses Semaphore v4 ceremony so any structural change there must be validated against ceremony compatibility

winner.title: Define JWT-based off-chain session token format for verified handshakes
winner.description: After a successful on-chain handshake verification, there is no standardized way to carry the result into off-chain API calls. Define a JWT profile (RFC 7519) where the payload includes the humanNullifier, agentNullifier, sessionNonce, scopeCommitment, and chain of delegation nullifiers. The JWT is signed by the relayer/verifier and has a short TTL (e.g., 5 minutes). This lets services accept Bolyra auth results without querying on-chain state per request, and gives framework integrations (LangChain, Vercel AI) a standard bearer token to pass. Deliverable: a spec document in spec/ with ABNF for the JWT claims, a reference TS implementation in sdk/, and 10+ test vectors.

outline.title: Define JWT-based off-chain session token format for verified handshakes

artifacts.description: JWT profile spec with ABNF claim definitions, signing requirements, TTL policy, and delegation chain encoding
Reference TS implementation: issueSessionToken(), verifySessionToken(), and claim type definitions
TypeScript interfaces: BolyraJWTPayload, SessionTokenOptions, VerifiedSessionClaims
10+ test vectors covering valid tokens, expired TTL, tampered claims, delegation chain permutations, and scope commitment mismatches
Integration guide for LangChain and Vercel AI SDK middleware to extract and validate Bolyra bearer tokens

steps: Audit existing handshake output shape (humanNullifier, agentNullifier, sessionNonce, scopeCommitment) in sdk/src/ and contracts/ to confirm canonical field names and byte encodings
Define JWT header: alg ES256 (or EdDSA for Ed25519 relayer keys), typ 'bolyra+jwt'
Define JWT payload ABNF: bolyra-claims = humanNullifier / agentNullifier / sessionNonce / scopeCommitment / delegationChain / iss / sub / iat / exp / chainId / verifierContract
Specify delegationChain as an ordered JSON array of nullifier hex strings (root → leaf), empty array for non-delegated sessions
Set TTL policy: default exp = iat + 300s (5 min), max 3600s, relayer MUST reject tokens with exp - iat > 3600
Specify claim validation rules: scopeCommitment must equal Poseidon(permissions, salt) committed on-chain; chainId must match the verified contract's network
Implement issueSessionToken(handshakeResult, relayerPrivKey, opts) in sdk/src/session-token.ts using jose library
Implement verifySessionToken(token, relayerPubKey) with full claim validation and expiry check

Write 10+ test vectors: (1) valid minimal token, (2) valid with 3-hop delegation chain, (3) expired token rejection, (4) wrong alg header, (5) tampered humanNullifier, (6) scopeCommitment mismatch, (7) chainId mismatch, (8) delegationChain out of order, (9) max TTL boundary, (10) missing required claim, (11) future iat rejection
Export issueSessionToken and verifySessionToken from sdk/src/index.ts public API surface
Write integration guide showing Express middleware pattern and LangChain/Vercel AI bearer token extraction
Add spec to spec/ index and link from sdk/QUICKSTART.md
dependencies: RFC 7519 (JWT)
RFC 7518 (JWA — ES256/EdDSA)
jose npm package (^5.x) for JWT sign/verify
Bolyra handshake output types from sdk/src/types/
On-chain verifier contract address + chainId from contracts/deployments/
Poseidon hash implementation (circomlibjs) for scopeCommitment recomputation in verifier

winner.title: Publish machine-readable conformance test vector suite
winner.description: No independent implementation can validate correctness without canonical test vectors. Create a spec/test-vectors/ directory with JSON files covering: valid handshake (human + agent), expired agent credential, stale Merkle root (both trees), scope subset violation in delegation, cumulative bit encoding violation (bit 4 without bits 2+3), delegation chain at max depth (3 hops), nonce replay rejection, and revoked human nullifier. Each vector includes all circuit inputs, expected public outputs, and expected contract revert reason. This is table-stakes for any IETF submission. Deliverable: 30+ test vectors in JSON with a conformance runner script in spec/conformance/.

outline.title: Publish machine-readable conformance test vector suite

artifacts.description: Valid human+agent handshake with all circuit inputs, public outputs, and expected verification result
Valid handshake using a delegated agent credential (1-hop delegation)
Agent credential past expiry timestamp — expected: verifier rejects
HumanUniqueness proof against a root no longer accepted by registry — expected: contract revert STALE_ROOT
AgentPolicy proof against a stale agent registry root — expected: contract revert STALE_AGENT_ROOT
Delegation grants permission bit not present in parent credential — expected: circuit constraint failure
FINANCIAL_UNLIMITED (bit 4) set without FINANCIAL_SMALL (bit 2) and FINANCIAL_MEDIUM (bit 3) — expected: validateCumulativeBitEncoding rejects
FINANCIAL_MEDIUM (bit 3) set without FINANCIAL_SMALL (bit 2) — expected: validateCumulativeBitEncoding rejects
3-hop delegation chain at maximum allowed depth — all hops valid, expected: verification passes
4-hop delegation chain exceeding max depth — expected: contract revert MAX_DELEGATION_DEPTH
Reuse of (humanProof, agentProof) pair with previously consumed sessionNonce — expected: contract revert NONCE_ALREADY_USED
HumanUniqueness proof whose nullifierHash is on the on-chain revocation list — expected: contract revert NULLIFIER_REVOKED
Agent with only READ_DATA (bit 0) performing a read-scoped handshake — expected: passes
Agent with all 8 bits set (valid cumulative encoding) — expected: passes
humanProof and agentProof carry mismatched nonceBinding values — expected: verifier rejects
humanMerkleRoot in proof differs from root committed in agentCredential — expected: circuit constraint failure
Delegation reduces permissions from 0xFF to 0x01 — expected: circuit passes, narrow scope enforced
Delegation attempts to expand permissions beyond parent — expected: circuit constraint failure
AgentPolicy proof with modelHash that doesn't match registered operator commitment — expected: revert
Structurally invalid Groth16 proof (zeroed pi_a) — expected: verifyProof returns false
Schema documentation: field names, types, required fields, how to read expected_result and revert_reason
JSON Schema (draft-07) defining the structure every test vector must conform to
Node.js conformance runner: loads all vectors, generates witnesses, optionally runs full proofs (FULL_PROOF=1), checks expected outcomes, reports pass/fail per vector
Validates all JSON files in spec/test-vectors/ against schema.json before running — fails fast on malformed vectors
Hardhat-based harness that deploys verifier contracts locally and tests on-chain revert reasons for contract-layer vectors
How to run conformance suite, how to add new vectors, mapping from IETF draft sections to vector IDs, known implementation gaps

steps: Define and commit spec/test-vectors/schema.json with required fields: id, category, description, circuit_inputs, public_outputs, expected_result (pass|fail), revert_reason (nullable), proving_system (groth16|plonk|none)
Write spec/test-vectors/README.md documenting schema fields, category taxonomy (valid, invalid_circuit, invalid_contract, invalid_encoding), and how revert_reason maps to Solidity custom errors

Implement the 8 core valid-path vectors (valid_handshake_basic, valid_handshake_delegated, valid_delegation_chain_max_depth, valid_delegation_scope_narrowing, valid_read_only_agent, valid_full_permissions_agent, valid_revoked_then_reissued placeholder, valid_plonk_proving_system variant) populating all circuit inputs from known test secrets

Implement the 12+ invalid-path vectors covering: expired credential, stale human root, stale agent root, scope subset violation, cumulative bit violations (bit4 without 2+3, bit3 without 2), max depth exceeded, nonce replay, revoked nullifier, wrong nonce binding, mismatched human root, delegation scope expansion, wrong model hash, malformed proof
Implement spec/conformance/vector-validator.js using ajv to validate every JSON file against schema.json; exit non-zero on schema violations

Implement spec/conformance/runner.js: load vectors, for each run circom_tester witness generation to confirm circuit constraint satisfaction or violation matches expected_result; skip full proof by default
Extend runner.js with FULL_PROOF=1 mode: generate actual Groth16/PLONK proofs for valid vectors, call snarkjs groth16.verify / plonk.verify, assert verification result matches expected_result

Implement spec/conformance/contract-harness.js: deploy verifier + registry contracts via Hardhat in-process, submit proofs for contract-layer vectors, capture revert reasons, assert they match revert_reason field
Wire all three into package.json: add 'conformance:validate', 'conformance:fast' (witness only), 'conformance:full' (with proofs), 'conformance:contracts' targets
Add CI step in .github/workflows/ that runs conformance:validate + conformance:fast on every PR; gate conformance:full on 'slow-proofs' label
Write spec/conformance/CONFORMANCE.md mapping each vector ID to the relevant section of spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md and spec/did-method-bolyra.md
Verify total vector count >= 30; add supplemental vectors for edge cases (zero permissions agent, single-bit permission variants, boundary expiry timestamp = now) to reach target
Run full conformance suite end-to-end locally, confirm all pass/fail outcomes match expected_result, commit with DCO sign-off

winner.title: Register application/bolyra-proof+cbor media type and define proof envelope format
winner.description: The protocol currently has no canonical serialization for proof payloads exchanged between prover and verifier. Define a CBOR-based proof envelope (leveraging RFC 9052 COSE structure patterns) with fields for proof type (human/agent/delegation), public signals array, proof bytes (a/b/c points), protocol version, and an optional delegation chain. Register the application/bolyra-proof+cbor media type per RFC 6838. This is critical for interop: without a wire format, every integration reinvents serialization. Deliverable: spec/proof-envelope.md with CDDL schema, SDK encode/decode functions, and conformance test vectors.

outline.title: Register application/bolyra-proof+cbor media type and define proof envelope format

artifacts.description: Canonical specification for the proof envelope format: CDDL schema, field semantics, COSE structure alignment, versioning policy, and media type registration rationale per RFC 6838.

Machine-readable CDDL schema defining the top-level ProofEnvelope map, ProofType enum, PublicSignals array, Groth16Proof struct (a/b/c points as bstr), optional DelegationChain array, and protocolVersion uint.

TypeScript encode/decode functions: encodeProofEnvelope(envelope: ProofEnvelope): Uint8Array and decodeProofEnvelope(bytes: Uint8Array): ProofEnvelope using a minimal CBOR library (cbor-x). Validates required fields and version range on decode.
TypeScript type definitions for ProofEnvelope, ProofType enum (human | agent | delegation), Groth16ProofPoints, and DelegationChainEntry.
Round-trip encode/decode tests for all three proof types, delegation chain presence/absence, version mismatch rejection, and malformed CBOR rejection.

Conformance test vectors: hex-encoded CBOR bytes paired with canonical JSON representations for human proof, agent proof, delegation proof, and a chained delegation. Used by any implementation to verify interop.
Integration guide: how to attach application/bolyra-proof+cbor as a Content-Type header, consume the envelope in a verifier, and extend with custom fields using the reserved CBOR map key range.

steps: Survey existing serialization usage across sdk/src, integrations/, and spec/ to identify fields already in use (proof bytes layout, public signals ordering, nonce placement) so the envelope schema captures current reality.

Define the CDDL schema in spec/proof-envelope.cddl: top-level map with integer keys (0=protocolVersion uint, 1=proofType uint enum, 2=publicSignals [+ bstr], 3=proof {a: [bstr,bstr], b: [[bstr,bstr],[bstr,bstr]], c: [bstr,bstr]}, 4=delegationChain [* DelegationEntry], 5=sessionNonce bstr ?); reserve keys 16–31 for private extensions.

Write spec/proof-envelope.md covering: COSE alignment rationale (why envelope-not-Sign1), media type string (application/bolyra-proof+cbor), IANA registration template (RFC 6838 §4.2), canonicalization rules (definite-length encoding, sorted integer keys), versioning policy (protocolVersion=1 is this spec, decode MUST reject unknown major versions).
Add cbor-x as a dependency in sdk/package.json (runtime dep, ~6 kB minified); audit for supply-chain risk and pin to exact version in overrides if a transitive advisory exists.
Implement sdk/src/types/envelope.ts with ProofType enum, Groth16ProofPoints interface, DelegationChainEntry interface, and ProofEnvelope interface; export from sdk/src/index.ts.

Implement sdk/src/envelope.ts: encodeProofEnvelope validates all required fields, serializes to CBOR map with integer keys, returns Uint8Array; decodeProofEnvelope parses CBOR, validates protocolVersion range, checks required keys, throws typed errors (EnvelopeDecodeError subclassing BolyraError).

Generate conformance test vectors by running encodeProofEnvelope on known fixture inputs (reuse existing sdk/test fixtures for proof points and public signals), then hex-encode and store in spec/conformance/proof-envelope-vectors.json alongside the canonical JSON representation.

Write sdk/test/envelope.test.ts covering: round-trip for human/agent/delegation types, delegation chain with two entries, encode→decode identity, decoding a vector from the conformance file, rejection of future major version (protocolVersion=255), rejection of truncated CBOR, rejection of missing required key.
Run npm run test:circuits:fast and npm run test:contracts to confirm no regressions from the new sdk dependency.
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md and integrations/mcp/README.md to reference the new media type and show a minimal encode/decode snippet.
Commit all artifacts with DCO sign-off (git commit -s); include a note in CHANGELOG.md under [Unreleased] for the new proof-envelope module and media type.
dependencies: cbor-x (or equivalent deterministic CBOR library) as a new sdk/ runtime dependency
RFC 6838 media type registration template (informational — no blocking external process for pre-standard use)
RFC 9052 COSE structure patterns (informational reference for envelope design)
Existing sdk/src/index.ts public API surface (encode/decode must be additive exports)
Existing proof point byte layout from circuits/build .zkey artifacts (defines bstr lengths in the CDDL)
spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md (must cross-reference the new envelope spec)

winner.title: Complete DID method resolution algorithm with revocation and delegation discovery
winner.description: The existing did-method-bolyra.md defines the DID scheme but lacks a complete resolution algorithm per W3C DID Core §7.1. Specifically: (1) resolving did:bolyra:<commitment> must query the IdentityRegistry to check enrollment and revocation status, (2) the DID Document should include a verificationMethod referencing the on-chain Merkle root and a service endpoint for proof submission, (3) delegation relationships should appear as delegateOf entries linking to the delegator's DID. Without this, no DID resolver can implement the method. Deliverable: updated spec/did-method-bolyra.md with resolution pseudocode, error handling for revoked/unknown DIDs, and 5 resolution test vectors (enrolled human, enrolled agent, revoked, delegated, unknown).

outline.title: Complete DID method resolution algorithm with revocation and delegation discovery

artifacts.description: Updated DID method spec with W3C DID Core §7.1-compliant resolution algorithm, pseudocode, error codes, and 5 resolution test vectors
Standalone resolution algorithm document: input/output types, registry query steps, revocation check, delegation discovery, DID Document construction
5 canonical resolution test vectors: enrolled human, enrolled agent, revoked identity, delegated credential, unknown DID
Extend IdentityRegistry with revocationStatus(commitment) view and delegateOf(commitment) view to support resolver queries
TypeScript DID resolver implementing the resolution algorithm against IdentityRegistry, returns W3C DIDResolutionResult
Unit + integration tests for all 5 resolution test vector cases using Hardhat local node
Operator guide: how to run a did:bolyra resolver node, configure RPC endpoint, interpret resolution errors

steps: Audit spec/did-method-bolyra.md to identify gaps against W3C DID Core §7.1 (resolution input, metadata, error codes, DID Document structure)
Define the canonical resolution input: did:bolyra:<commitment>, optional resolutionOptions (versionId, accept media type)

Specify IdentityRegistry query sequence: (1) parse commitment from DID, (2) call isEnrolled(commitment), (3) call revocationStatus(commitment), (4) call delegateOf(commitment) to discover delegation chain
Add revocationStatus(bytes32 commitment) view and delegateOf(bytes32 commitment) view to IdentityRegistry.sol; emit RevocationStatusChanged and DelegateRegistered events
Define DID Document JSON-LD structure: id, verificationMethod (type BolyraZKProofKey2026, controller, onChainMerkleRoot), authentication array, service endpoint for proof submission API
Write pseudocode resolution algorithm covering: valid DID → enrolled → revoked → delegated → unknown, with W3C error codes (invalidDid, notFound, representationNotSupported, internalError)
Define delegateOf DID Document extension: add delegateOf relationship array linking to delegator DID with commitment reference and scope hash
Author 5 resolution test vectors as JSON with input DID, expected DIDDocument or error, and resolutionMetadata fields
Implement TypeScript resolver in sdk/src/resolver.ts using ethers.js to call IdentityRegistry view functions and construct DIDResolutionResult
Write Hardhat integration tests for all 5 vectors: deploy IdentityRegistry, seed test state (enroll, revoke, delegate), run resolver, assert output matches test vector
Update spec/did-method-bolyra.md with the algorithm section, error table, and cross-references to test vectors and resolver SDK
Write docs/did-resolver-quickstart.md covering npm install, RPC config, resolver instantiation, and error handling patterns

spec/did-method-bolyra.md (existing DID scheme definition)
W3C DID Core §7.1 resolution specification

winner.title: Self-describing proof envelope with named public signals
winner.description: Proofs are currently bare `{ proof, publicSignals: bigint[] }` with positional semantics — `publicSignals[0]` vs `publicSignals[4]` is a constant source of integration bugs. Ship a `BolyraEnvelope` type with `encode()`/`decode()` that wraps proofs in `{ version, circuit, provingSystem, signals: { humanMerkleRoot, nullifierHash, ... }, proof }`. Include a `fromRaw(circuit, rawSignals)` migration helper. Publish in both TS and Python SDKs so integrators never index by position again.

outline.title: Self-describing proof envelope with named public signals

artifacts.description: Canonical specification for BolyraEnvelope wire format: version, circuit identifier, provingSystem enum, named signals schema per circuit, and codec contract
Core BolyraEnvelope type, encode()/decode() functions, fromRaw(circuit, rawSignals) migration helper, and per-circuit signal maps (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation)

Exported const maps from circuit name to ordered signal name arrays, e.g. HUMAN_UNIQUENESS_SIGNALS = ['humanMerkleRoot', 'nullifierHash', 'nonceBinding']. Single source of truth for positional indexing.
Re-export BolyraEnvelope, encode, decode, fromRaw from public API surface
Python dataclass BolyraEnvelope with encode()/decode() (JSON-serializable dict), fromRaw() helper, and signal maps mirroring the TS definitions
Unit tests: round-trip encode/decode for all three circuits, fromRaw migration helper, version mismatch rejection, unknown circuit rejection, missing signal detection
Python pytest suite: round-trip, fromRaw, cross-compatibility with TS-encoded envelopes (JSON fixtures shared)
Shared JSON fixture files with canonical encoded envelopes for all three circuits, used by both TS and Python test suites to verify cross-SDK compatibility
Add BolyraEnvelope section showing migration from positional publicSignals[] to named signals, with fromRaw() one-liner upgrade path

steps: Define the BolyraEnvelope wire format in spec/proof-envelope-v1.md: version string (semver), circuit enum ('HumanUniqueness' | 'AgentPolicy' | 'Delegation'), provingSystem enum ('groth16' | 'plonk'), signals record keyed by name, opaque proof field

Create sdk/src/circuits/signal-maps.ts with ordered name arrays for each circuit derived from the authoritative public signal ordering in circuits/src/*.circom; add a lint check that signal count matches
Implement BolyraEnvelope TypeScript type and encode() in sdk/src/envelope.ts: accepts (circuit, provingSystem, rawProof, rawSignals[]) and returns versioned envelope with named signals

Implement decode() in sdk/src/envelope.ts: validates version, circuit, provingSystem fields; maps named signals back to bigint[]; throws typed errors (EnvelopeVersionError, UnknownCircuitError, SignalCountMismatch)
Implement fromRaw(circuit, provingSystem, rawProof, rawSignals[]) as a thin alias for encode() to serve as the migration helper for existing integrators using positional arrays
Wire BolyraEnvelope into existing proveHandshake() and verifyHandshake() return types so the SDK natively emits envelopes; keep a rawMode flag for backward compat

Mirror the spec in sdk-python/bolyra/envelope.py using dataclasses: BolyraEnvelope dataclass, encode() returns JSON-serializable dict, decode() accepts dict, fromRaw() helper; signal maps as module-level constants
Write shared JSON fixture files (sdk/test/fixtures/envelope-v1-samples.json) capturing canonical encoded envelopes for all three circuits with known signal values
Write TS unit tests in sdk/test/envelope.test.ts covering: round-trip for all circuits, fromRaw migration, version mismatch, unknown circuit, signal count mismatch, JSON serialization fidelity
Write Python pytest suite in sdk-python/tests/test_envelope.py loading the shared JSON fixtures to verify TS-encoded envelopes decode correctly in Python and vice versa
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with a migration guide: before/after code showing positional publicSignals[] replaced by named envelope fields, fromRaw() one-liner
Bump sdk package version (minor) and sdk-python version; update CHANGELOG entries for both packages
dependencies: circuits/src/HumanUniqueness.circom (authoritative public signal order)
circuits/src/AgentPolicy.circom (authoritative public signal order)
circuits/src/Delegation.circom (authoritative public signal order)
sdk/src/index.ts (existing public API surface to extend)
sdk-python/bolyra/__init__.py (existing Python public API)

winner.title: Prove no field-overflow on public signal round-trip to Solidity
winner.description: The circuits use Num2Bits(64) for permissionBitmask, expiryTimestamp, and currentTimestamp, but sessionNonce, scope, modelHash, and requiredScopeMask have NO range checks — they are full field elements (< BN254 r ≈ 2^254). The Solidity verifier passes these as uint256, so values near r could alias modularly. Add Num2Bits(253) constraints on all unranged public inputs to guarantee they are canonical field elements, and document in FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md which signals are range-checked and to what width. This closes the class of 'modular aliasing' attacks where a malicious prover submits p + x instead of x.

outline.title: Prove no field-overflow on public signal round-trip to Solidity

artifacts.description: Add Num2Bits(253) range constraints on sessionNonce, scope, modelHash, requiredScopeMask public inputs
Add Num2Bits(253) range constraints on sessionNonce public input; audit nullifierHash and humanMerkleRoot for existing Poseidon-implied bounds
Add Num2Bits(253) range constraints on any unranged delegatee public signals; verify requiredScopeMask is already covered by cumulative-bit check or add explicit range
New shared template file exporting RangeCheck(n) wrapper around Num2Bits(n) to avoid duplicating the include across all circuits
Add table documenting every public input signal, its circuit, its range-check width (64, 253, or Poseidon-implied), and the attack class each constraint closes
Mocha tests: valid boundary values (0, 2^253-1) pass witness generation; value == BN254 r causes constraint failure; each circuit rejects p+x aliased inputs
Short prose explaining the modular aliasing attack class, which signals were unranged, and the fix applied — links to FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md

steps: Audit every public signal across HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, and Delegation: list signal name, current range constraint (if any), and how it reaches the Solidity verifier as uint256

Identify Poseidon-output signals (humanMerkleRoot, nullifierHash, credentialCommitment) — Poseidon internally reduces mod r, so these are already canonical; document this as 'Poseidon-implied < r' in the spec, no Num2Bits needed
For signals with existing Num2Bits(64) (permissionBitmask, expiryTimestamp, currentTimestamp) confirm they already prevent aliasing at their declared width; no change needed
Create circuits/src/RangeChecks.circom with a parameterized template RangeCheck(n) { signal input in; component b = Num2Bits(n); b.in <== in; } for reuse

In AgentPolicy.circom, include RangeChecks.circom and instantiate RangeCheck(253) for sessionNonce, scope, modelHash, requiredScopeMask; wire each public signal through the component before it is used in any other constraint
In HumanUniqueness.circom, add RangeCheck(253) for sessionNonce; verify nonceBinding is derived from sessionNonce inside the circuit (so checking the input is sufficient)
In Delegation.circom, add RangeCheck(253) for any delegatee-supplied signals not already covered by the cumulative-bit Num2Bits check
Run npm run compile:circuits and confirm R1CS constraint counts increase by exactly 253 per added check (one Num2Bits(253) = 253 constraints)
Write range_checks.test.js: for each circuit, test witness generation with in=0, in=2^253-1 (both must succeed), in=BN254_r (must throw), and in=BN254_r+1 (must throw)
Run npm run test:circuits:fast to confirm all new and existing tests pass
Update circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md: add 'Public Signal Range Guarantee' table with columns Signal | Circuit | Range | Method | Attack Class Closed
Write docs/security/field-overflow-mitigation.md explaining the vulnerability class, affected signals, and remediation
Run npm run test:contracts to confirm Solidity verifier integration tests still pass with new zkey artifacts after recompile
Update estimated_constraints in this outline with actual delta observed after compile
dependencies: circomlib Num2Bits template (already a project dependency)
pot16.ptau — new AgentPolicy and Delegation zkeys must be regenerated after adding constraints; HumanUniqueness reuses Semaphore ceremony so its zkey must also be rebuilt if signals are added
snarkjs trusted setup re-run for project-specific circuits (AgentPolicy, Delegation) using existing pot16.ptau
Solidity verifier contracts must be regenerated from new vkey.json after zkey rebuild
npm run compile:circuits must complete successfully before test suite can run
